repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1 Theorem 1: inclusion and equality boundary

published_prior_art.cite: PlagborgMollerWolf2021: common-restriction LP/VAR equivalence; set-inclusion support monotonicity is routine

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1 Theorem 2: conditional quantitative margin theorem

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: PN2/PN5 only partially delivered: the mechanism objects S_w, C_w, tau_w^* are named, but the lock pivots the parent from partial-ID support comparison to exact-ID and the parent reduction redefines tau rather than deriving it from the declared mechanism delta.

novelty_flags.label: proposal
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless tau_w^* is made into a genuinely derived, computable threshold rather than lambda_w(P) C_w(P) with lambda_w assumed.

soundness_flags.label: Assumption regular-contact / Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The modulus says rotations near the exposed face with residual at least c lose at least lambda c in support value, but exposed-face rotations themselves have zero support loss and can have residual C_w(P)>0, so the assumption is self-contradictory as stated.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary reduction to parent margin
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed equality tau_w^*(P)=chi_w(P) does not reduce from tau_w^*=lambda_w C_w; it replaces the exposed-face definition by the parent epsilon-envelope and silently redefines the threshold.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked block declares an exact-identification cluster, but the setup and results compare support functions over feasible rotation sets, matching the parent's partial-identification support-bound cluster.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Relock the proposal as a partial-identification/support-function upgrade, or explain why the support-set comparison is exact identification.
Replace Assumption regular-contact with an error-bound statement about contact-locus rotations or NG-feasible rotations losing support relative to the exposed LP face.
Redefine tau_w^* as the actual support loss to a cumulant-contact envelope, then prove the parent chi_w corner as an algebraic specialization rather than an asserted equality.
Add a two-shock worked boundary case showing C_w(P)>0, Delta_w(P)>0, and the threshold value explicitly; this would make the mechanism non-tautological.

recommended_next_step: Redraft the same angle with the cluster lock fixed and the contact-threshold theorem rebuilt around a non-contradictory error-bound assumption.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The draft names support-contact mechanism objects, but the cross-cluster lock and invalid tau-to-parent reduction prevent the mechanism upgrade from being delivered cleanly.
